summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a dynamic show with standout Scarlet Begonias, The Other One and Fire, despite minor start‑up roughness.
review: The recording is praised as a full, dynamic SBD source with crisp USB quality, only hampered by a brief mic dropout and a rough beginning to the first two tracks. Musically the show shines – the opening trio of Scarlet Begonias/Space, the ethereal transition into Fire, and a soaring performance of The Other One are highlighted as superb moments.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and describe both the performance and soundboard recording in glowing terms; only one low star rating is unrelated to music quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Full, dynamic SBD with crisp USB mix; minor mic blink‑out on Bob's vocal and a rough start to the first two tracks noted but overall very clear.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong second‑set opening trio and seamless transitions (e.g., VOTC into Fire) are praised; the set is described as a smooth, energetic cruise.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia delivers gorgeous jams, takes full control in the middle of The Other One and leads the band with confidence.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh provides a "bomb" at 5:27 and locks in solidly with heavy tribal drumming support.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir's vocal reading on The Other One is highlighted as a pleasure; he also attempts a brief solo.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince contributes backup vocals on Fire; Hornsby adds a short keyboard solo.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (drums) offers heavy tribal rhythms that drive the performance.
